differentials.name: mild localized pain
differentials.raw: mild localized pain (in 4% of unexplained wrist pain)

differentials.name: mucoid degeneration of intraosseous connective tissue
differentials.raw: mucoid degeneration of intraosseous connective tissue (perhaps due to trauma / ischemia)

differentials.name: penetration of juxtaosseous soft-tissue ganglion (= synovial herniation) into underlying bone (occasionally) Path:  uni- / multilocular cyst surrounded by fibrous lining, containing gelatinous material [proximal tibia] [medial malleolus] [femoral head] [carpal bones]
differentials.raw: penetration of juxtaosseous soft-tissue ganglion (= synovial herniation) into underlying bone (occasionally) Path:  uni- / multilocular cyst surrounded by fibrous lining, containing gelatinous material

context: Origin:   coalescence of smaller cysts formed by myxomatous degeneration of periarticular connective tissue Path:   capsule of dense fibrous connective tissue Histo:   no synovial lining (frequent lack of communication with joint, infrequent association with joint / tendon sheath effusion) Cause:   repetitive stress Location:   joint capsule, tendon sheath, ligament, bursa, intraarticular, subchondral intraosseous, periosteal Prognosis:   symptomatic; bone erosion; spontaneous resolution frequent in pediatric cases DDx:   Synovial cyst (differentiation from ganglion cyst radiologically not pos
